title: juncaginaceae
intro: The Juncaginaceae rank is a family of flowering plants, specifically a group of aquatic and terrestrial plants characterized by their unique seed and flower structures. Juncaginaceae members typically exhibit strap-shaped or cordate leaves and produce small white or yellowish flowers in clusters. This family comprises about 45 species, primarily found in wetland environments worldwide, including bogs, marshes, and aquatic habitats. Examples of Juncaginaceae include the hornwort and the sea club-rush.
